ambiguities.aspect: Allocation of purchase price between land and building
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: The transaction states the land and building were acquired 'for the purpose of constructing a new factory' and 'the existing building will be demolished immediately' — the building has no future economic benefit, so the entire $60,000,000 purchase price is attributable to land under IAS 16 cost principles
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 16.16 requires cost to include all expenditures necessary to bring the asset to the condition and location for its intended use — since the building will be demolished immediately and the purpose is land for a new factory, demolition is part of land preparation and the entire cost is land

ambiguities.aspect: Present value calculation for deferred payment
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: The transaction states a $60,000,000 purchase price paid in 6 annual instalments of $10,000,000 at 6% market rate — this is a financing arrangement requiring present value measurement under IAS 16.23
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 16.23 requires that when payment is deferred beyond normal credit terms, the cost is the cash price equivalent (present value), with the difference recognized as interest over the credit period

ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: 'At the beginning of the year, Ford acquired' and 'commencing at the end of the year of acquisition' — the transaction describes the acquisition event, requiring initial recognition at present value
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 16 requires recognition when control passes — 'acquired' indicates control has transferred, triggering initial recognition

flags.aspect: Present value calculation of deferred payment stream

best_attempt.reason: Recording land at stated purchase price without present value adjustment, with liability at face value

flags.gap: Requires present value calculation of 6-period annuity at 6% (PV = $10M × 4.9173 = $49,173,240), with $10,826,760 difference as financing cost to be amortized — mathematical computation beyond text-based reasoning
